name: RSA
creator: Rivest, Shamir, Adleman (MIT)
breakability: 2048-bit: no known practical attack. 1024-bit: potentially factored with nation-state resources.
modern_use: TLS key exchange, digital signatures, SSH keys, email encryption (PGP/S-MIME)
description: RSA is the most widely used asymmetric encryption algorithm. Security relies on the computational difficulty of factoring large integers. Two keys: a public key for encryption, a private key for decryption. RSA-OAEP with SHA-256 is the modern secure padding scheme.
notes: Always use OAEP padding. PKCS1v15 padding is vulnerable to padding oracle attacks. RSA is slow - in practice used to encrypt symmetric keys, not bulk data.
did_you_know: The RSA algorithm was independently discovered by British GCHQ mathematician Clifford Cocks in 1973, but kept classified for 24 years.
